description.p: Northstar is a clean-room web browser written from scratch in C, with a GTK 4 user interface and a libcurl network stack. It is built to be small, secure, and readable by a single person end to end.

ul.li: A from-scratch HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript engine — no forked browser engine.
One window, one page, one process: the page engine runs in the shell process behind a seccomp + Landlock syscall sandbox on Linux.
No JIT, which keeps the JavaScript attack surface small.
No telemetry: it does not phone home and does not track the user.
Dropdown (select) controls render their chosen option again, and native checkboxes no longer draw doubled with author CSS decorations.
Fixed a heap-corruption crash when closing the browser after using the bookmarks popover.
Updated the vendored Wuffs image decoders and the WAMR WebAssembly interpreter (CVE-2026-54913 loader fix).
New about:start splash artwork; refreshed Debian/RPM packaging; new Alpine (musl) and macOS CI builds.

description.p: Initial release of the Northstar GPL edition: a single-window, single-process desktop browser for Linux and Windows.
